events.title: Weather tracker: Heatwave expected to bring Germany and Poland to 40C
events.description: Austrian Grand Prix declares heat hazard as dramatic rise in temperatures forecast in central and northern Europe Temperatures are forecast to rise dramatically in parts of central and northern Europe this weekend as the intense heatwave continues . In Germany and Poland, highs up to or exceeding 40C (104F) are expected on both Saturday and Sunday, days after swathes of France experienced similar extreme temperatures. The Austrian Grand Prix, taking place this weekend in Spielberg, has declared a heat hazard , the first race to do so this season. Temperatures are expected to rise into the low 30s celsius during the race, almost 10C warmer than usual for the venue. Across Europe, the intense heat has led to severe thunderstorms. In the Sverdlovsk region of central Russia, a strong tornado was reported on the evening of 22 June. Rated a 3 out of 5 on the international Fujita scale, the twister injured 16 people and destroyed or damaged about 100 homes and businesses in the town of Kushva. Emergency services have been working on restoring power to the town. Tornadoes are not unheard of in Russia but are rare. Forecasters say they may become more common in future as the climate breaks down. Storm warnings remained in effect in the southern part of the country on Friday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Heatwave expected to bring Germany and Poland to 40C

events.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Father and son rescued as international teams help search

Another 3,200 ​people were injured ⁠and 3,100 ​left homeless ​by the ​disaster, ​the National Assembly president added, speaking ​on ​state television. Continue reading...

events.title: Venezuela quake death toll reaches 920 as interim president vows to save ‘as many as possible’
events.description: Delcy Rodríguez says foreign rescue teams are arriving as anger grows at official response and limited resources Survivors tell of ‘brutal and fast’ Venezuela quake as hunt for survivors goes on Venezuela’s interim president, Delcy Rodríguez, has vowed to fight to save “as many people as possible” as the official death toll from the country’s worst earthquake in more than a century almost doubled, but frustration was growing at the perceived sluggishness of the government’s response. Rodríguez’s brother, Jorge, the president of the national assembly, said on Friday that the official number of dead had risen to 920. Delcy Rodríguez had earlier said almost 3,000 people were injured. Speaking during a tour of La Guaira, the most devastated region , she said foreign search and rescue groups were starting to arrive. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venezuela quake death toll reaches 920 as interim president vows to save ‘as many as possible’

The discovery of survivors in Caraballeda was made by French and American rescue teams nearly four days after back-to-back quakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 struck, completely destroying nearly 200 buildings in the area. events.title: Venezuelan earthquakes test Trump’s new western hemisphere policy after gutting of USAID
events.description: Marco Rubio is scrambling to provide effective disaster response to country whose president US deposed in January This week’s dual earthquakes in Venezuela are a test for the new era of American power in the western hemisphere, as the Trump administration scrambles to provide an effective disaster response mission to a country that it now calls an ally in Latin America, after a US special forces raid in January deposed the country’s strongman leader, Nicolás Maduro. The US is marshalling what secretary of state Marco Rubio called a “big, fast, effective” and “whole-of-government” response as the state department sent three specialised urban search and rescue teams and pledged a $150m assistance fund that one former disaster relief expert called the largest he had seen within 24 hours of an incident. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venezuelan earthquakes test Trump’s new western hemisphere policy after gutting of USAID

The patient was transferred to a specialist facility and was in a stable condition, the ministry said in a statement . “All precautionary measures, including the patient’s isolation, were taken upon his arrival in the country, with transfer to the hospital under secure conditions to prevent any risk of contamination.” Continue reading...

events.description: Cape Verdeans in Britain feeling ‘incredibly proud’ after team’s hard-fought draws against Spain and Uruguay World Cup live – latest updates For as long as she can remember, 13-year-old Lauryn struggled to find a map that included Cape Verde. Now, to her great delight, the tiny African island nation is finally centre stage. “Seeing our country shown across the world at the World Cup makes me feel incredibly proud,” Lauryn says. “After the first match, everyone was talking about Cape Verde. People saw the talent and the skill of our players.” Continue reading...

events.title: Australia to double potential fines over child social media accounts
events.description: Australia plans to double potential fines for social media platforms, including Facebook and Instagram, who fail to prevent Australian children from holding accounts.

events.title: EIB announces largest-ever loan: €3 billion to Airbus
events.description: The European Investment Bank (EIB) has said the loan, the largest in its history, is aimed at helping boost the EU plane maker's research efforts. The money will be directed to commercial and defense projects.

events.title: Australia-Vanuatu deal bars foreign bases in island nation
events.description: Australia and Vanuatu have signed a deal seen as a bid to check China's growing security presence in the region. But an economic deal between Vanuatu and China is still in the works.

events.title: Pakistan's Geo News apologizes for Muharram broadcast
events.description: The broadcaster Geo News was handed a 15-day suspension for a documentary that was "liable to hurt the religious sentiments of viewers," Pakistan's media watchdog said.

events.title: Fact check: As Europe bakes, should you trust your sunscreen?
events.description: As a heat wave sweeps across Europe, claims are resurfacing online questioning whether sunscreen is safe or linked to skin cancer risk.

events.title: With 'Operation Purgatory,' Magyar moves to demolish Orban system
events.description: The new Hungarian government is moving swiftly to dismantle the system of former Prime Minister Viktor Orban. The focus: fighting corruption, reforming the media and protecting democracy.

events.title: Temperature records tumble as heatwave moves east
events.description: Europe's deadly heatwave pushed east Sunday with hundreds of millions still sweltering across the continent and heat records broken in numerous countries, including Germany, Poland and the Czech Republic.

events.title: Man charged with murder after girl's body found in suitcase in Thailand
events.description: An Australian man has been charged with the murder of a teenage girl in Thailand after her body was found in a suitcase by police on Saturday.

events.title: Malaysia extends search for MH370 for another year, keeping families’ hopes alive
events.description: Malaysia has extended its contract with Ocean Infinity for another year to search for wreckage of Malaysia Airlines flight 370

events.title: Thai airline employee arrested in Australia on suspicion of importing heroin
events.description: Australian police have arrested a Thai airline employee for allegedly importing over a kilogram of heroin into Melbourne

events.title: A long-awaited Australia-Vanuatu pact blocks China from building a military base
events.description: Australia and Vanuatu have signed a long-awaited bilateral security and economic treaty that prevents China creating a military base on the South Pacific island nation

events.title: China imposes export controls on 40 Japanese entities as tensions with Tokyo rise
events.description: China has imposed new export controls on 40 Japanese entities, accusing them of contributing to Japan’s “remilitarization.”

events.title: Europe's record heat has overwhelmed Paris mortuaries and left families in distress
events.description: A record-breaking heat wave has overwhelmed mortuaries in Paris, leaving funeral directors struggling to find space for bodies

events.title: A Chinese dissident recounts his perilous dinghy escape to South Korea and how he got to Canada
events.description: Chinese dissident Dong Guangping has finally reached Canada after a perilous escape from China

events.title: Australia to double potential fines for Facebook and Instagram over child social media accounts
events.description: Australia plans to double potential fines for social media platforms, including Facebook and Instagram, who fail to prevent Australian children from holding accounts

events.title: Ugandan army chief orders the closure of a major news platform
events.description: Uganda's military chief and son of President Yoweri Museveni has ordered the shutdown of a major news organization

events.title: Iraqi officials, including lawmakers, arrested on corruption charges
events.description: Iraqi political officials have been arrested on corruption charges

events.title: Guterres urges renewed commitment to multilateralism on UN Charter Day
events.description: Secretary-General António Guterres and General Assembly President Annalena Baerbock reaffirmed the principles outlined in the UN Charter and called for a renewed commitment to multilateralism at a General Assembly meeting on Friday marking the Charter’s 81st anniversary.

events.title: Security Council extends critical stabilisation force in Syria
events.description: The 15-member Security Council unanimously adopted a draft resolution on Thursday renewing the mandate of the UN Disengagement Observer Force (UNDOF) for six months.

events.title: Fox News Campus Radicals Newsletter: Parents take schools to court, drag queen school board member arrested
events.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News Campus Radicals newsletter.

events.title: Man dead, another critical after double stabbing at Brooklyn park as police detain person of interest: NYPD
events.description: A 21-year-old man died and a 30-year-old remains in critical condition after a double stabbing at Dyker Beach Park in Brooklyn on Saturday afternoon.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Man dead, another critical after double stabbing at Brooklyn park as police detain person of interest: NYPD

events.title: Parents of 7-year-old who died weighing 255 pounds charged with murder in suspected neglect case
events.description: Parents of a 7-year-old boy who died weighing 255 pounds face murder charges after prosecutors say the child was bedridden and living in filth.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Parents of 7-year-old who died weighing 255 pounds charged with murder in suspected neglect case

events.title: Women accuse Pearadise founder of preying on them by turning ‘safe space’ into mansion of manipulation
events.description: A viral body positivity community called Pearadise faces allegations of predatory behavior by its founder, sparking a legal battle and a new docuseries.

events.title: Minnesota man captured in Somalia after allegedly helping orchestrate $250M child nutrition fraud scheme
events.description: Abdikerm Abdelahi Eidleh was captured in Mogadishu after allegedly helping orchestrate the $250 million "Feeding Our Future" fraud scheme in Minnesota.

events.title: As America turns 250, a rare 1790 exchange between Washington and Bishop Carroll takes on new relevance
events.description: A 1790 letter from Bishop John Carroll to George Washington sought reassurance that Catholics would be equal citizens in the new American republic.

events.title: Farage says mass migration has changed the UK ‘literally beyond recognition,’ believes party can win election
events.description: Nigel Farage calls Britain's political system "completely broken" after Starmer's resignation and says Reform UK would have every chance of winning.
